reasoning: The Lean statement ∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal precisely captures the informal claim: "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The hypothesis `card G = 351` matches "a group of order 351". The existential binds both a prime p (via p.Prime), the divisibility condition p ∣ card G (matching "dividing its order"), a Sylow p-subgroup P (Mathlib's Sylow p G structure), and its normality via P.Normal (which is Sylow.Normal, i.e., the underlying subgroup is a normal subgroup). The quantifier structure, types, hypotheses, and conclusion all align with the informal statement without adding or dropping constraints.
